rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical radical chalcogenation/cyclization of N-tosyl alkenyl amines with diaryl dichalcogenides (PhXXPh, X = S, Se, Te) to give 2-(chalcogenylmethyl)pyrrolidines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl dichalcogenide PhXXPh to generate PhX• radical (and/or PhX+); the chalcogenyl radical adds to the terminal alkene, and the resulting carbon radical undergoes intramolecular cyclization with the tethered N-tosyl amine

key_intermediates.name: phenylchalcogenyl radical PhX• (X = S, Se, Te)
carbon-centered radical from anti-Markovnikov addition of PhX• to terminal alkene of N-tosyl pent-4-enylamine

electron_flow: At the anode (GF) the diaryl dichalcogenide is oxidized to give PhX•, which adds to the terminal alkene of the N-tosyl pent-4-enylamine; the resulting secondary C-radical cyclizes onto the N-tosylamide nitrogen (with loss of H+, reduced at the GP cathode to H2) to deliver the 2-substituted pyrrolidine.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: With 2 equiv TEMPO, formation of S-product 4 was suppressed to trace, while Se (32, 70%) and Te (47, 47%) products still formed, indicating partial radical involvement (chalcogen-dependent)

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: DPE intercepted PhX• radicals giving (2,2-diphenylvinyl)(phenyl)chalcogenides I (S, 30%), II (Se, 10%), III (Te, trace), while cyclized pyrrolidines were still obtained (4, 15%; 32, 65%; 47, 49%), supporting a PhX• radical intermediate

notes: Radical trapping behavior is chalcogen-dependent: the S pathway is most sensitive to TEMPO/DPE (strong radical character), whereas Se and Te reactions are only partially inhibited, possibly implying a competing two-electron/ionic (PhX+) pathway for the heavier chalcogens. Cyclic voltammetry (HFIP/TBABF4, GC working electrode) was used to probe redox potentials of PhXXPh; CV of 1,2-diphenylditellane was measured in TFE due to poor HFIP solubility.
